evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor opens the BlackLab index — documented as intentional: 'opening the index is inherent to BlackLab's lifecycle model'; constructor declares throws IOException so callers get a checked exception rather than a deferred runtime failure
WordSketchApiServer constructor builds all handler instances (SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, etc.) but defers port binding to start() — correct separation of construction and network I/O
Main.handleBlackLabServerCommand: shutdown hook added before server construction — ensures executor is always closed even if server construction throws; comment explicitly explains this ordering
GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish() reads System.getProperty at call time, not at class load — no class-initialization side effects
HttpApiUtils.setCorsHeader() reads System.getProperty at call time — per-call reads allow per-test overrides to take effect immediately, documented in code
SemanticFieldExplorer constructor initializes three fields (comparator, multiSeedExplorer, nounCqlConstraint) with no I/O — pure construction
CollocateQueryHelper accepts @Nullable index — allows test construction without I/O but introduces the partial-guard issue noted in type_safety
WordSketchApiServer has two constructors: convenience constructor creates SemanticFieldExplorer internally; full constructor accepts injected explorer for testing — clean DI seam without global state
No static initializer blocks with I/O or side effects found in any production class
No global mutable singletons found — all state is held in instance fields with clear ownership
ConlluConverter: static Pattern MWT_OR_EMPTY compiled at class load — benign, patterns are compile-time constants
initialization_coupling.issues_preventing_higher_score: BlackLabQueryExecutor performs I/O in its constructor (opening the index), which is documented as intentional but means the object cannot be constructed without a live index. This prevents mock-construction without subclassing and makes construction order visible to callers.

strengths: Shutdown hook is registered before server construction, preventing resource leaks on construction failures
Port binding deferred to start() in WordSketchApiServer — construction and network binding are cleanly separated
No static initializer blocks with I/O or mutable global singletons anywhere in the codebase
System.getProperty reads are deferred to call time (not class load), enabling per-test overrides without JVM restart
initialization_coupling.issue_character: Initialization coupling is minimal and well-controlled; the only structural coupling is that BlackLabQueryExecutor performs I/O in its constructor by design, which is documented and intentional given BlackLab's lifecycle model.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: The initialization story is mature: construction vs binding separation in WordSketchApiServer, deferred property reads in HttpApiUtils, and explicit shutdown-hook ordering in Main all reflect deliberate design decisions. The constructor I/O in BlackLabQueryExecutor is the only non-ideal pattern but is correctly documented and unavoidable given the library contract.
